contraindications: avoid during pregnancy and breastfeeding unless clinician-guided
Use caution with prescription medications and chronic conditions; monitor for intolerance.
safetyNotes: Contraindicated in hypersensitivity to thyme or other Lamiaceae; warnings to seek medical evaluation if dyspnoea, fever, or purulent sputum occurs; pregnancy/lactation safety not established (not recommended).

sources.title: Community herbal monograph on Thymus vulgaris L. and Thymus zygis L., herba (EMA)

description: EMA monograph covers multiple liquid/dry extracts and comminuted herb as herbal tea, all for oral use.
dosage: EMA provides multiple preparation-specific regimens; examples include liquid extracts (e.g., DER 1:1 single dose 1–2 mL, 3–4 times daily) and other extracts/tinctures with variable dosing (max daily doses depend on preparation).

preparation: Oral use as herbal tea (comminuted herb) and as liquid/dry extracts/tinctures; ethanol-containing tinctures/extracts require appropriate labeling guidance.
region: Native range of *Thymus vulgaris* (POWO): SW Europe and SE Italy.
therapeuticUses: historical use for symptom relief in regional folk medicine
sideEffects: mild gastrointestinal upset (nausea, cramping, or loose stool)
allergic rash or itching in sensitive individuals
summary: Traditional herbal medicinal product used in productive cough associated with cold, exclusively based on long-standing use (traditional-use basis).
